message:
Did you know your dog has a THIRD eyelid? 🐶👁️ It's not a myth — it's real, and it's quietly doing important work every single day.

Tucked away in the inner corner of each eye (the side nearest the nose) sits a thin, pale membrane vets call the nictitating membrane — or simply the "haw." Most of the time you'll never see it, because it stays neatly folded out of sight.

So what does it actually do? Quite a lot. Like a tiny built-in windshield wiper, it sweeps across the eye to clear away dust, pollen and grit. It helps spread tears evenly to keep things comfortable. And it even comes with its OWN tear gland — one that produces up to half of all the tears your dog's eye needs.

The main time it peeks out is when your pup is drifting off to sleep or just waking up. Ever caught your dog dozing with a spooky little sliver of pale "eye" showing? 😅 That's the third eyelid saying goodnight — totally normal.

(One gentle note: if you see it up and covering the eye while your dog is wide awake, that can be a sign something's irritating them, and it's worth a quick chat with your vet.)

Had you ever spotted your dog's third eyelid before today? 🐾
